source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design the primary structural chassis of a CanSat to fit a 350 ml beverage-can envelope (OD 66 mm x height 130 mm) and survive 20 G deployment shock in all three axes, delivered as a multi-part assembly because the CanSat chassis is by design a built-up structure composed of an outer shell with an RF window, internal decks hosting FR4 PCBs, captive component mounts for electronics/battery, and a parachute pack interface - all of which participate in the shock pass/fail, component-displacement, and modal-decoupling checks.

Assembly layout. (A) Outer shell: Al 6061-T6 sheet/machined cylindrical shell approx 66 mm OD x 130 mm tall with at least one RF window/slot for the 430 MHz antenna (no full conductive enclosure allowed around antenna). (B) Internal decks: FR4 PCB decks stacked inside the shell, holding GPS, IMU, power, flight computer. (C) Captive component mounts: bracketry retaining battery, parachute pack, or paraglider/wheel axle depending on class, ensuring < 2 mm shift under 20 G. (D) Deployer interface: carrier-rocket deployer rail or ejection-spring interface at the aft end. (E) Antenna window: RF-transparent panel (FR4/plastic) covering the slot for the 430 MHz antenna.

Material. Al 6061-T6 (E = 68.9 GPa, Fty = 276 MPa, rho = 2700 kg/m^3); FR4 for decks; polymer / FR4 for RF window.

Load cases. LC1 (axial 20 G deployment shock). LC2 (lateral Y 20 G). LC3 (lateral Z 20 G).

Pass/fail criteria (assembly-level). (R1) Envelope fit <= 66 mm OD. (R2) Envelope height <= 130 mm. (R3) Total mass <= 1050 g (Open class cap). (R4) Max internal component displacement under LC1/LC2/LC3 <= 2.0 mm. (R5) First modal frequency >= 100 Hz (rocket motor burn decouple).

Assembly policy. Envelope fit (R1, R2) is a whole-chassis geometric check on the assembled stack; mass budget (R3) sums all part families; component displacement (R4) depends on captive-mount stiffness and shell-deck coupling; first mode (R5) is the assembled-chassis modal quantity. No part evaluated alone has meaningful pass/fail.

Solver and analysis expectations. Primary: linear static FEM for 20 G loads (R4) with shell elements for outer shell, shell for PCB decks, and RBE2/lumped-mass for electronics. Free-free or deployer-constrained modal for R5. Thermal, fluid, ionizing-radiation NOT required.

Deliverables. STEP AP242 of CanSat chassis assembly, mass budget per family, total mass in g, max bracket stress at 20 G axial in MPa, max component displacement in mm, and first modal frequency in Hz.
short_prompt:
Design the primary structural chassis of a CanSat to fit a 350 ml beverage-can envelope (OD 66 mm x height 130 mm) and survive 20 G deployment shock in all three axes, delivered as a multi-part assembly: Al 6061-T6 outer shell (E = 68.9 GPa, Fty = 276 MPa, rho = 2700 kg/m^3) with an RF window/slot for a 430 MHz antenna + FR4 PCB internal decks (GPS, IMU, power, flight computer) + captive component mounts (battery, parachute pack, paraglider/wheel) + deployer interface + RF-transparent antenna window.

Load cases.
- LC1 axial 20 G deployment shock.
- LC2 lateral Y 20 G.
- LC3 lateral Z 20 G.

Pass limits: OD <= 66 mm; height <= 130 mm; total mass <= 1050 g (Open class cap); max internal component displacement under LC1/LC2/LC3 <= 2.0 mm; first modal frequency >= 100 Hz (rocket motor burn decouple).

prompt.title: Small-form-factor CanSat structural chassis as multi-part assembly
prompt.brief: 350 ml beverage-can envelope CanSat: Al outer shell + FR4 decks + captive component mounts + deployer interface + RF window; 20 G deployment shock, <1050 g, 430 MHz telemetry.

parts.name: outer_shell
parts.role: Al 6061-T6 cylindrical outer shell enclosing the internal stack

parts.name: internal_deck
parts.role: FR4 PCB deck hosting avionics / battery

parts.name: captive_mount
parts.role: bracket retaining battery / parachute pack / paraglider riser or wheel axle

parts.name: deployer_interface
parts.role: carrier-rocket deployer rail / ejection-spring interface at the aft end

parts.name: antenna_window
parts.role: RF-transparent window/panel covering the 430 MHz antenna slot

boundary_conditions.mounting: carrier-rocket deployer rail / ejection spring interface fixed

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Decouple from rocket motor burn frequency
Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

interfaces.load_path: deck inertia reacted into shell under 20 G
battery/parachute inertia constrained to < 2 mm shift
non-structural; ensures RF transparency at 430 MHz

fem_check_policy.rationale: Envelope fit, mass, component displacement under deployment shock, and first modal frequency all reference the assembled shell + decks + mounts + deployer. No part evaluated alone has meaningful pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part_is_inherent: CanSat structural chassis is the assembly containing electronics, battery, parachute, and payload with captive mounts; the rulebook mass cap and deployment-shock checks are whole-assembly metrics.

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Short flight, no explicit thermal gradient target in rules.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Parachute dynamics treated by kinematic sizing, not CFD.

eval_coverage: tight envelope fit across assembled stack
20 G deployment shock with captive mounts
first modal frequency above burn band on full chassis
RF window requirement at shell-window interface

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
